meta.page_title: Shadow Work Quiz - Meet Your Hidden Self | DopaBrain
meta.description: What shadow self lurks beneath your surface? People Pleaser, Perfectionist, Controller, Avoider, Rebel, or Caretaker? 8 questions reveal your hidden shadow!
meta.keywords: shadow work quiz, shadow self test, Jungian shadow test, shadow work journal, inner shadow quiz, repressed self test, psychology quiz, self-discovery test, shadow archetype, Carl Jung shadow
meta.og_title: Shadow Work Quiz - Meet Your Hidden Self
meta.og_description: 8 questions reveal your shadow archetype! Discover which hidden pattern drives your behavior.
meta.twitter_title: Shadow Work Quiz - Meet Your Hidden Self

question.0: When a friend takes your help for granted, what's your first reaction?
question.0a: Say it's fine and swallow my frustration
question.0b: Blame myself for not setting better expectations
question.0c: Set conditions so I stay in control of the situation
question.0d: Avoid the conversation and deal with it later
question.0e: Express my frustration directly and set a boundary
question.0f: Try to understand their perspective while feeling drained
question.1: After making a mistake in an important presentation, how do you cope?
question.1a: Pretend nothing happened and hope no one noticed
question.1b: Replay the mistake over and over, criticizing myself
question.1c: Vow to prepare so perfectly next time it can't happen again
question.1e: Remind myself that mistakes are okay and move on
question.1f: Worry more about how others felt than my own feelings
question.2: When someone close to you says something hurtful?
question.2a: Pretend it didn't hurt to keep the peace
question.2b: Analyze what I did wrong to deserve it
question.2c: Try to regain the upper hand in the relationship
question.2e: Honestly tell them they hurt me
question.2f: Try to understand their pain while putting my feelings aside
question.3: When a colleague takes credit for your idea in a team project?
question.3a: Let it go — conflict isn't worth it
question.3b: Try to come up with an even better idea to prove myself
question.3c: Make sure I present my own work next time to claim credit
question.3d: Lose motivation for team projects entirely
question.3e: Speak up immediately and clarify my contribution
question.3f: Tell myself it's fine as long as the team succeeds
question.4: When you have free time alone on a weekend, what's your first thought?
question.4a: I should reach out to someone and make plans
question.4b: I should catch up on work or self-improvement
question.4c: Plan and organize everything for the coming week
question.4d: I don't want to do anything — just lie down
question.4e: Do whatever I feel like and enjoy the freedom
question.4f: Think about what I could do for my family or friends
question.5: When strong emotions (anger, sadness, jealousy) arise, what's your pattern?
question.5a: Smile and act like nothing's wrong
question.5b: Judge myself as immature for feeling this way
question.5c: Find the cause and try to control the situation
question.5d: Distract myself to forget the emotion
question.5e: Feel the emotion fully and express it authentically
question.5f: Focus on how everyone else is feeling instead of myself
question.6: When you receive criticism or negative feedback?
question.6a: Agree with a smile while crumbling inside
question.6b: Vow to address every point perfectly so I never hear it again
question.6c: Question the critic's authority or motives
question.6d: Push it aside and focus on something else
question.6e: Accept what's valid and dismiss what isn't
question.6f: Worry about how the person giving feedback felt
question.7: Late at night, reflecting on yourself, what thought comes up most?
question.7a: I'm afraid people would be disappointed if they knew the real me
question.7b: There's always a feeling that I'm not enough
question.7c: It makes me anxious that I can't control everything
question.7d: Everything feels exhausting and I want to escape this discomfort
question.7e: I accept myself as I am, imperfections and all
question.7f: I worry whether I'm doing enough for the people I care about

result.percentile: Only {pct} of people share the {type} archetype

peoplePleaser.name: The People Pleaser
peoplePleaser.tagline: A mask that hides the real you behind others' expectations
peoplePleaser.description: Your shadow is suppressed anger and boundaries. An unconscious fear that saying 'no' means being unloved keeps you performing the role of the 'nice one.' But authentic relationships begin when you show your real emotions. Your anger is a signal pointing to where your boundaries need to be.
peoplePleaser.prompt: "When was the last time I genuinely said 'no'? What emotion came up when I did?"

perfectionist.name: The Perfectionist
perfectionist.tagline: Armor that hides vulnerability behind a flawless facade
perfectionist.description: Your shadow is suppressed vulnerability. An unconscious belief that mistakes erase your worth keeps you on the endless treadmill of self-improvement. But real growth begins when you embrace imperfection. You are already enough.
perfectionist.prompt: "If I could be loved even without being perfect, what would I dare to try?"

controller.name: The Controller
controller.tagline: A fortress built on control instead of trust
controller.description: Your shadow is suppressed trust and surrender. An unconscious fear that letting go means falling apart keeps you constantly on guard. But paradoxically, accepting that you can't control everything is when you find true stability.
controller.prompt: "When I actually let go of control, what was the worst that happened? Was it truly unbearable?"

avoider.name: The Avoider
avoider.tagline: A fog that chooses safe numbness over painful truth
avoider.description: Your shadow is suppressed pain and confrontation. An unconscious fear that feeling uncomfortable emotions will break you keeps you in your safe zone. But hidden in the things you avoid are your greatest opportunities for growth.
avoider.prompt: "What conversation, emotion, or decision am I most avoiding? What am I losing by avoiding it?"

rebel.name: The Rebel
rebel.tagline: A wild flame that chooses freedom over belonging
rebel.description: Your shadow is suppressed belonging and conformity. An unconscious fear that following rules means losing yourself keeps you in constant resistance. But true freedom comes not from opposing for the sake of opposing, but from choosing what you genuinely want.
rebel.prompt: "Am I truly against this, or do I just hate the feeling of being controlled?"

caretaker.name: The Caretaker
caretaker.tagline: An emptiness behind the mask of caring for everyone but yourself
caretaker.description: Your shadow is suppressed self-needs. An unconscious belief that prioritizing yourself is selfish keeps you endlessly giving to others. But you can't pour from an empty cup. Self-care isn't selfishness — it's survival.
caretaker.prompt: "If I didn't have anyone to take care of, who would I be? Does my identity exist without caregiving?"

share.text: My shadow archetype is '{type}'! What's your hidden self?
